channel.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories
channel.description: Top stories in the U.S. and world news, politics, health, science, business, music, arts and culture. Nonprofit journalism with a mission. This is NPR.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

image.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories

item.title: Army reviewing after helicopters hovered alongside Kid Rock's swimming pool as he saluted
item.description: The Army has launched an administrative review after two AH-64 Apache helicopters on a training run hovered near the hillside home of Kid Rock as the outspoken supporter of President Donald Trump saluted their crews.
item.content:encoded:
The Army has launched an administrative review after two AH-64 Apache helicopters on a training run hovered near the hillside home of Kid Rock as the outspoken supporter of President Donald Trump saluted their crews.

(Image credit: John Amis)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Airport cleared to be renamed for Trump as he unveils design for skyscraper library
item.description: A Florida airport was cleared to be renamed after President Donald Trump on Monday, hours before the president revealed plans for a Miami skyscraper planned to house his presidential library.
item.content:encoded:
A Florida airport was cleared to be renamed after President Donald Trump on Monday, hours before the president revealed plans for a Miami skyscraper planned to house his presidential library.

(Image credit: Mark Schiefelbein)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Women's Final Four is set, and it is a repeat of last season's
item.description: No. 1 seeds UConn, UCLA, Texas and South Carolina are in the Final Four for the second straight season, just the second time the same teams have reached the sport's final weekend in consecutive years.
item.content:encoded:
No. 1 seeds UConn, UCLA, Texas and South Carolina are in the Final Four for the second straight season, just the second time the same teams have reached the sport's final weekend in consecutive years.

(Image credit: Justine Willard)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Thieves steal paintings by Renoir, Cézanne and Matisse from a private museum in Italy
item.description: Thieves made off with three paintings by Renoir, Cézanne and Matisse worth millions of euros from a museum near the city of Parma in northern Italy.
item.content:encoded:
Thieves made off with three paintings by Renoir, Cézanne and Matisse worth millions of euros from a museum near the city of Parma in northern Italy.

(Image credit: Domenico Stinellis)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: BTS rampages back to the top of the charts
item.description: The K-pop group has officially returned from its four-year hiatus bigger than ever. Based solely on first-week sales, there's only one artist who has done any better.
item.content:encoded:
The K-pop group has officially returned from its four-year hiatus bigger than ever. Based solely on first-week sales, there's only one artist who has done any better.

(Image credit: Todd Owyoung/NBC)
item.dc:creator: Stephen Thompson

item.title: Epstein survivor responds to lawsuit over release of identifying information
item.description: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with Epstein survivor Danielle Bensky about a new class action lawsuit against the Justice Department and Google over the release of identifying information about victims.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with Epstein survivor Danielle Bensky about a new class action lawsuit against the Justice Department and Google over the release of identifying information about victims.
item.dc:creator: Kathryn Fink

item.title: Yemeni politician says former U.S. soldiers tried to kill him. Now he's suing in U.S. court
item.description: The suit is centered around the alleged attempt on Anssaf Ali Mayo's life. But it raises broader questions, including about the role of the United Arab Emirates in Yemen's civil war.
item.content:encoded:
The suit is centered around the alleged attempt on Anssaf Ali Mayo's life. But it raises broader questions, including about the role of the United Arab Emirates in Yemen's civil war.

(Image credit: Mohammed Huwais)
item.dc:creator: Ryan Lucas

item.title: Trump rolls back pause on asylum decisions imposed after D.C. National Guard shooting
item.description: The Homeland Security Department has lifted its total ban on reviewing asylum applications, a pause that affected millions of cases. The pause remains in effect for about 40 countries.
item.content:encoded:
The Homeland Security Department has lifted its total ban on reviewing asylum applications, a pause that affected millions of cases. The pause remains in effect for about 40 countries.

(Image credit: Carlos Moreno)
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo

item.title: U.S. could exempt oil industry from protecting Gulf animals, for 'national security'
item.description: National security has never been used to call a meeting of the "God Squad." But other federal agencies have been citing the "energy emergency" to avoid rules meant to protect endangered animals.
item.content:encoded:
National security has never been used to call a meeting of the "God Squad." But other federal agencies have been citing the "energy emergency" to avoid rules meant to protect endangered animals.

(Image credit: NOAA/SEFSC)
item.dc:creator: Chiara Eisner

item.title: Figure skating season ends with redemption and heartbreak. What do fans watch next?
item.description: Worlds marks the last competition of the 2025-2026 season. Skaters have some time to go on tour, rest up and learn new routines before the next season starts in July.
item.content:encoded:
Worlds marks the last competition of the 2025-2026 season. Skaters have some time to go on tour, rest up and learn new routines before the next season starts in July.

(Image credit: Michal Cizek)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman
